description_long: This is a vintage-style gas station with a prominent art deco architectural design, featuring a tall cylindrical tower with a vertical 'CONOCO' sign illuminated in green. The main building has a flat roof and a covered fueling area supported by decorative columns, with two classic fuel pumps at the front. The exterior walls are primarily cream-colored with yellow accents, and large windows are visible on the side. Several oil drums and tires are scattered around the perimeter, adding to the station's retro roadside ambiance. The ground is marked with a bold 'Route 66' emblem, emphasizing its historic highway location. The overall structure is solid and substantial, with a mix of concrete, metal, and glass materials.
description: A retro gas station with an art deco tower, two vintage fuel pumps, and Route 66 markings, surrounded by oil drums and tires. The building features cream and yellow walls, large windows, and a prominent 'CONOCO' sign.
description_view_0: Front view showing two vintage fuel pumps, the main entrance, Route 66 ground marking, and the tall 'CONOCO' tower.
description_view_1: Side angle highlighting the tower, canopy structure, and scattered oil drums and tires near the fueling area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying large windows, yellow side wall, and the back of the tower with more oil drums and tires.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the side of the canopy, yellow door, and additional oil drums and tires near the building.
attribute_fuel_pumps: Two vintage-style fuel pumps are present at the front of the station, each with a rounded top, visible hoses, and nozzles. The pumps are primarily cream-colored with green accents and have retro design elements, including circular display dials. No digital displays or modern branding are visible.
attribute_canopy_structure: A rectangular canopy covers the fueling area, supported by two decorative columns at the front corners. The canopy matches the building's cream color and features art deco trim and a central triangular sign. Subtle lighting fixtures are integrated into the canopy's underside.
attribute_station_signage: The most prominent signage is the tall vertical 'CONOCO' sign atop the cylindrical tower, illuminated in green. Additional triangular 'Conoco' logos are mounted above the fuel pumps and on the canopy. No other directional or safety signage is visible.
attribute_convenience_store_entrance: The main entrance is located at the front, beneath the canopy, featuring a single manual door with a transom window above. The entrance is flanked by the two fuel pumps and is marked by a small overhang and art deco trim. No specific convenience store branding is visible above the entrance.
